items.question: Which group lists only immutable Python types?
items.explanation: int, str and tuple are immutable. list, dict and set are mutable, so any option containing them is wrong.

items.question: What does the expression "3" + 5 produce in Python?
items.explanation: Python is strongly typed and will not coerce a str and an int. It raises TypeError: can only concatenate str (not "int") to str.

items.question: What is the result of isinstance(True, int)?
items.explanation: bool is a subclass of int in Python, so a bool is also an int. (True == 1 as well.)

items.question: Are Python type hints enforced by the interpreter at runtime?
options: No, they are informational and used by static tools
items.answer: No, they are informational and used by static tools
items.explanation: Hints (PEP 484) are metadata for readers and tools like mypy. The interpreter does not check or coerce them.
